article_title: IREN Acquires Mirantis for $625 Million in All-Stock Deal, Adding Kubernetes and OpenStack Software to Its AI Cloud Stack

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: IREN Acquires Mirantis for $625 Million in All-Stock Deal, Adding Kubernetes and OpenStack Software to Its AI Cloud Stack
content_preview.summary: Former bitcoin miner IREN will pay $625 million in shares for Mirantis, the long-standing OpenStack and Kubernetes vendor behind the k0rdent platform, layering enterprise infrastructure software on top of its 2 GW Sweetwater AI campus.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

IREN Limited announced on May 5, 2026 that it has signed a definitive agreement to acquire Mirantis, the cloud infrastructure software company best known for commercializing OpenStack and, more recently, the Kubernetes-based k0rdent AI platform. The transaction is structured as an all-stock deal valued at approximately $625 million in IREN ordinary shares at signing, [according to IREN's announcement on GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/05/3287514/0/e...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong News piece, 837 words. Clear structure (Overview, What We Know, executive framing, open-source commitments, What We Don't Know, Analysis). Disciplined attribution.

source_verification.source-0_globenewswire: Verified verbatim: $625M all-stock deal, Mirantis as standalone subsidiary, customary regulatory closing conditions, 1,500+ enterprise customers, NVIDIA AI Cloud Ready Initiative ISV partner, k0rdent AI platform spans bare metal/VMs/Kubernetes, Daniel Roberts Co-CEO quote, Alex Freedland Founder/CEO quote 'Mirantis has spent more than a decade helping enterprises deploy and manage cloud infrastructure'. Also corroborates Sweetwater 1 energization via the related IREN release.
source_verification.source-1_siliconangle: Verified: $625M all-stock value, $220M prior funding from Intel + Goldman Sachs, Iris Energy 2018 origin and 2024 rebrand, $9.7B Microsoft contract / 200 MW capacity in November 2024, Sweetwater 1,800-acre / 2 GW / 700,000 liquid-cooled Nvidia GPU capacity, k0rdent platform components (Services Controller, CAPI, GPU virtualization, inference autoscaling), Shaun O'Meara CTO quote 'Our contributions to k0rdent, Kubernetes, k0s, OpenStack and more continue' / 'k0rdent remains open source'.
source_verification.source-2_yahoofinance: Snapshot fetched but landed on consent.yahoo.com Italian consent gate. The single claim attributed to Yahoo (Sweetwater 1 energization 'this week') is independently corroborated via the related IREN GlobeNewswire press release (source-0) which links 'Announces Successful Energization of Sweetwater 1', 1.4GW Sweetwater 1 data center site, May 1 2026. Yahoo consent-gate failure does not affect verifiability.

editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All quoted material traced verbatim to source snapshots. The Yahoo redirect is a known consent-gate pattern; the cited claim is corroborated.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. The Analysis section labels strategic interpretation explicitly and identifies the open-source community concern as the metric to watch.
editor_notes.originality: No prior IREN / Mirantis / k0rdent archive coverage. Fresh story.
concerns: Yahoo Finance source-2 redirected to consent.yahoo.com Italian consent gate. The Sweetwater 1 energization claim is corroborated via the IREN GlobeNewswire snapshot.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Cryptography, sources, and content all clean. Yahoo consent-gate redirect is an info-level finding only.
